migrations.description: Rename DataPipeline to DataFlow for tenants already at 3.0.1 without migration
Rename DataPipeline to DataFlow for tenants where previous migration failed
Merge EdgeAdapter/MeshAdapter into Adapter, EdgePipeline/MeshPipeline into Pipeline
Merge EdgeAdapter/MeshAdapter into Adapter, EdgePipeline/MeshPipeline into Pipeline (retry after engine fix)

scripts.description:
Migration from System.Communication 3.0.1 to 3.0.2.

Renames DataPipeline CK type to DataFlow for tenants that were
updated to 3.0.1 before the migration script existed.

Breaking change: The CK type ID changes from
System.Communication/DataPipeline to System.Communication/DataFlow.

steps.description: Change CkTypeId from DataPipeline to DataFlow for all existing DataPipeline entities

postValidations.description: Ensure no entities with legacy DataPipeline type exist after migration
Verify that DataFlow entities were created from migrated DataPipeline entities

scripts.description:
Migration from System.Communication 3.0.2 to 3.0.3.

Renames DataPipeline CK type to DataFlow for tenants where the
previous migration attempt failed or was skipped.

steps.description: Change CkTypeId from DataPipeline to DataFlow for all existing DataPipeline entities

postValidations.description: Ensure no entities with legacy DataPipeline type exist after migration
Verify that DataFlow entities were created from migrated DataPipeline entities

scripts.description:
Migration from System.Communication 3.0.3 to 3.1.0.

This migration unifies the adapter and pipeline type hierarchies:
- EdgeAdapter and MeshAdapter are merged into Adapter
- EdgePipeline and MeshPipeline are merged into Pipeline
- DataPipelineTrigger is renamed to PipelineTrigger

All existing EdgeAdapter/MeshAdapter entities become Adapter entities.
All existing EdgePipeline/MeshPipeline entities become Pipeline entities.
All existing DataPipelineTrigger entities become PipelineTrigger entities.

steps.description: Change CkTypeId from EdgeAdapter to Adapter for all EdgeAdapter entities
Change CkTypeId from MeshAdapter to Adapter for all MeshAdapter entities
Change CkTypeId from EdgePipeline to Pipeline for all EdgePipeline entities
Change CkTypeId from MeshPipeline to Pipeline for all MeshPipeline entities
Change CkTypeId from DataPipelineTrigger to PipelineTrigger

scripts.description:
Migration from System.Communication 3.1.0 to 3.1.1.

This migration unifies the adapter and pipeline type hierarchies:
- EdgeAdapter and MeshAdapter are merged into Adapter
- EdgePipeline and MeshPipeline are merged into Pipeline
- DataPipelineTrigger is renamed to PipelineTrigger

All existing EdgeAdapter/MeshAdapter entities become Adapter entities.
All existing EdgePipeline/MeshPipeline entities become Pipeline entities.
All existing DataPipelineTrigger entities become PipelineTrigger entities.

steps.description: Change CkTypeId from EdgeAdapter to Adapter for all EdgeAdapter entities
Change CkTypeId from MeshAdapter to Adapter for all MeshAdapter entities
Change CkTypeId from EdgePipeline to Pipeline for all EdgePipeline entities
Change CkTypeId from MeshPipeline to Pipeline for all MeshPipeline entities
Change CkTypeId from DataPipelineTrigger to PipelineTrigger
Update association references from DataPipeline to DataFlow

types.description: An external client that connects via SignalR to execute data pipelines. Adapters manage pools and receive pipeline configurations from the Communication Controller.
Configuration for AI services (e.g. Anthropic Claude API). Stores API credentials and default model settings. Referenced by pipeline nodes via ApiKeyConfigurationName.
A logical grouping of pipelines and triggers that belong together. DataFlows organize the data integration logic within a tenant.
Maps a source entity attribute to a target entity attribute with optional expression transformation
Abstract base type for entities that can be deployed to adapters or pools. Tracks deployment state, name, description, and status messages.
Configuration for Discord bot integration used by the ToDiscord pipeline node (POST /channels/{id}/messages).
IMAP email receiver configuration for polling a mailbox
SMTP email sender configuration for outgoing email delivery.
Configuration for EDA integration
Configuration for an energy community
Configuration for the finAPI banking adapter connection.
Configuration for Grafana integration. Used by the GrafanaProvisionTenant and GrafanaDeprovisionTenant pipeline nodes.
Configuration for connecting to a Loxone Miniserver via REST API
Microsoft Graph API configuration for OAuth2 client credentials authentication
A data processing pipeline that defines a sequence of processing nodes. Pipelines are deployed to adapters for execution and can be organized within data flows.
Records an individual pipeline execution with its status, timing, trigger type, and optional input/output data. Linked to the executed pipeline and the adapter that performed the execution.
Aggregated execution statistics for a pipeline across multiple time windows (1h, 12h, 24h, 30d). Tracks success/failure counts and average duration per period.
A scheduled trigger that executes one or more pipelines based on a cron expression. Organized within data flows alongside the pipelines they trigger.
A group of managed devices or entities. Pool operators register via SignalR to handle device communication within the pool.
The SAP configuration type is used to configure the connection to an SAP system.
Client credentials for service-to-service authentication (OAuth2 client credentials grant)
Configuration for connecting to an SFTP server. Supports password and private key authentication.
A label that can be associated with any entity for categorization and filtering purposes.

associationRoles.description: Links a pipeline execution record to the pipeline that was executed.
Links a pipeline to the adapter that executes it. Each pipeline is executed by exactly one adapter.
Links a pipeline execution record to the adapter that performed the execution.
Links an adapter to the pools it manages. A pool can be managed by at most one adapter.
Links a DataPointMapping to its source data point entity
Links a DataPointMapping to its target domain entity
Links a data source entity to a target domain entity for value propagation
Links a pipeline to downstream pipelines that receive its output data for chained processing.
Links a pipeline statistics record to the pipeline it aggregates metrics for. Each pipeline has at most one statistics record.
Links a tag to the entities it labels for categorization and filtering.
Links a pipeline trigger to the pipelines it starts on schedule.
Links a pipeline to the configurations it requires for execution (e.g. connection settings for external systems).

attributes.description: JSON-serialized adapter-specific configuration that is deployed to the adapter.
Grafana Server Admin password.
Grafana Server Admin username for organization management API.
AI model identifier to use (e.g. claude-sonnet-4-20250514, claude-haiku-4-5-20251001).
API key for authenticating with the AI service (e.g. Anthropic sk-ant-... key). Stored as configuration attribute, not exposed in pipeline data context.
Heading displayed in the application header bar
Browser tab title of the web application
The Azure Active Directory tenant ID (GUID) for OAuth2 authentication.
Background color of the web application
Name of the Telerik billing report template (e.g. SBEG_Billing.trdp)
The bot token used as the `Authorization: Bot {token}` header when calling the Discord REST API.
OAuth2 Client ID for authenticating with the external API.
OAuth2 Client Secret for authenticating with the external API.
Current SignalR connection state of the adapter or pool operator.
Timestamp of the last communication state change (online/offline transition).
Timestamp when execution completed
Current configuration deployment state of the adapter or pool.
Price per kWh for consumers in the energy community
Delay in days before automatic consumption record requests are sent
Current value of the data point as a string (numeric conversion happens during mapping evaluation)
Docker image name of the adapter container (e.g. octo-mesh-adapter).
Docker image version tag of the adapter container.
Current deployment state of the entity (undeployed, pending, deployed, or error).
Execution duration in milliseconds
ID of the energy community
Error message if execution failed
Unique identifier (GUID) for this execution
Current status of the pipeline execution (Running, Completed, Failed, Interrupted, Cancelled).
External identifier of the data point (e.g. Loxone State UUID, MQTT Topic, OPC-UA NodeId, Modbus Register)
Tax rate applied to farmers in the energy community
Favicon icon for the web application
The mailbox folder to monitor for incoming emails (e.g. INBOX).
End color of the footer gradient
Start color of the footer gradient
Icon for the app footer
Base URL of the Grafana instance.
The Discord guild (server) snowflake ID. Reserved for future channel-name resolution; not read by the ToDiscord node in v1.
End color of the header gradient
Start color of the header gradient
Icon for the app header
The host name is the name of the server on which the external system is running.
URL of the OctoMesh Identity Server for provisioned datasource OAuth configuration.
Optional input data (JSON) for debugging
Whether debugging is enabled for this pipeline
If true, the adapter connects to the sandbox environment instead of production.
The Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) protocol is used to secure communication between a client and a server.
URI of the OAuth2/OpenID Connect authorization server (e.g. https://identity.example.com)
Average execution duration in the last 12 hours (ms)
Failed executions in the last 12 hours
Successful executions in the last 12 hours
Average execution duration in the last 24 hours (ms)
Failed executions in the last 24 hours
Successful executions in the last 24 hours
Average execution duration in the last 30 days (ms)
Failed executions in the last 30 days
Successful executions in the last 30 days
Timestamp of the most recent execution
Average execution duration in the last hour (ms)
Failed executions in the last hour
Successful executions in the last hour
Last synced sequence number for offline sync
Timestamp of the last value update for this data point
When statistics were last calculated
Optional mXparser expression to transform the source value. Variable value contains the source value.
Resolved mapping targets for data acquisition. Populated automatically by the BuildMappingTargets pipeline node from DataPointMappings.
The maximum number of concurrent connections is the maximum number of connections that can be established with an external system at the same time.
Maximum number of tokens for the AI response.
URL of the OctoMesh MCP server for AI tool use (e.g. https://localhost:5017). The tenant ID is appended automatically.
Optional display name for the tag.
ID of the network partner (Marktpartner
The neutral color of the theme
OAuth client ID for the Grafana datasource plugin.
URL of the OctoMesh Asset Repository for provisioned datasource configuration.
Optional output data (JSON) from pipeline execution result
The password is the secret that is used to authenticate with the external system.
JSON-serialized pipeline node graph that defines the data processing logic.
Interval in seconds between polling cycles.
The port number is the number of the port on which the external system is listening for incoming connections.
The primary color of the theme
The private key is a secret key that is used in asymmetric encryption to authenticate with the external system.
The private key passphrase is a secret that is used to protect the private key.
Price per kWh for producers in the energy community
A client is a tenant instance in an SAP system. SAP is multi-tenant, which means that multiple organizations or departments can work on the same system without sharing data with each other.
An SAP gateway host is a server that provides communication services between SAP systems or between SAP systems and external applications.
An SAP gateway service provides communication services that are used between SAP systems or between SAP systems and external applications.
The language key is a two-character code that identifies the language in which the user interface is displayed.
A program ID is a unique identifier that is used to register a program on an SAP gateway server.
The registration count is the number of times a program is registered on an SAP gateway server.
The trace level is a number that determines the amount of information that is logged during communication with the SAP system.
The secondary color of the theme
Email address used as the sender (From) for outgoing emails.
Path to the source attribute to read (e.g., CurrentValue, or nested record.field). If not set, the adapter determines the default.
Identifier of the source entity (e.g. Loxone Control UUID, MQTT device ID)
Timestamp when execution started
Human-readable status or error message from the last deployment or configuration attempt.
The System ID (SID) is the unique technical name of an SAP system. It consists of three letters and uniquely identifies a system within a landscape.
The system number is a two-digit number assigned to an SAP system during installation. It is used to identify the SAP service (e.g. Dispatcher) on a specific port.
The tag value used for categorization and filtering.
Path to the target attribute to update on the mapped entity (e.g., Temperature, Humidity, or nested record.field)
Tax rate applied to energy transactions in the community
Temperature for AI response generation (0.0 = deterministic, 1.0 = creative).
The tertiary color of the theme
How this pipeline execution was initiated (Manual, Scheduled, Event, or Startup).
Theme color configuration for an application
The username is the name of the user that is used to authenticate with the external system.

records.description: Generic data point record for adapter data acquisition. Used in RecordArray attributes to store multiple named data points with their external identifiers and current values.
Resolved mapping target for data acquisition. Built from DataPointMappings by the BuildMappingTargets node. Contains the source identifier, optional state name, and the external ID to poll/subscribe.

enums.description: Indicates the current SignalR connection state of an adapter or pool operator.
Indicates whether a deployable entity has been successfully configured with its pipeline definitions and settings.
Tracks whether a deployable entity (adapter, pipeline, pool, or trigger) is currently deployed and active.
Status of a pipeline execution lifecycle, from running through completion or failure.
Indicates how a pipeline execution was initiated.

description: Construction kit model for the OctoMesh Communication Controller. Defines adapters, pipelines, pools, triggers, configurations, and execution tracking for data ingress and egress.
